repository.name: agency-engineering-skills
repository.description: Normative promises for the skill packages and the interface in this repository, assessed against the packaging, documentation, and architectural conventions the repository's own skills state about themselves.

/interface.title: The interface renders only previously generated data
/interface.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to render only data previously generated by a skill's own tooling, and OUGHT NOT recompute domain logic -- validation, inheritance, trust, or scoring -- in the browser.

/skills.title: Skills declare a manifest
/skills.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to declare a SKILL.md file at their root stating the skill's name, description, and operating instructions.

/skills.title: Skills document non-trivial workflows
/skills.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide reference documentation for any non-trivial workflow, rather than relying on SKILL.md alone.

/skills.title: Skills verify themselves automatically
/skills.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide an automated, repeatable way to check their own correctness -- a test suite or a bundled validator -- rather than depending on manual review alone.

/skills/hypothesize.title: Skills declare a manifest
/skills/hypothesize.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to declare a SKILL.md file at their root stating the skill's name, description, and operating instructions.

/skills/hypothesize.title: Skills document non-trivial workflows
/skills/hypothesize.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide reference documentation for any non-trivial workflow, rather than relying on SKILL.md alone.

/skills/hypothesize.title: Skills verify themselves automatically
/skills/hypothesize.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide an automated, repeatable way to check their own correctness -- a test suite or a bundled validator -- rather than depending on manual review alone.

/skills/hypothesize.title: Capability, evidence, and conclusion stay separate
/skills/hypothesize.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to keep capability status, evidence maturity, and scientific conclusion as three independent dimensions that are never conflated into one verdict.

/skills/ltp.title: Skills declare a manifest
/skills/ltp.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to declare a SKILL.md file at their root stating the skill's name, description, and operating instructions.

/skills/ltp.title: Skills document non-trivial workflows
/skills/ltp.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide reference documentation for any non-trivial workflow, rather than relying on SKILL.md alone.

/skills/ltp.title: Skills verify themselves automatically
/skills/ltp.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide an automated, repeatable way to check their own correctness -- a test suite or a bundled validator -- rather than depending on manual review alone.

/skills/ltp.title: Generated LTP artifacts are derived, not authored
/skills/ltp.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to treat every file under a generated/ directory as derived output that tooling regenerates, and OUGHT NOT hand-edit it.

/skills/promisify.title: Skills declare a manifest
/skills/promisify.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to declare a SKILL.md file at their root stating the skill's name, description, and operating instructions.

/skills/promisify.title: Skills document non-trivial workflows
/skills/promisify.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide reference documentation for any non-trivial workflow, rather than relying on SKILL.md alone.

/skills/promisify.title: Skills verify themselves automatically
/skills/promisify.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide an automated, repeatable way to check their own correctness -- a test suite or a bundled validator -- rather than depending on manual review alone.

/skills/promisify.title: Promises never store a verdict
/skills/promisify.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to keep promise declarations free of any verdict, trust, or compliance state; only assessments may record kept/broken/trust.

/skills/zpd.title: Skills declare a manifest
/skills/zpd.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to declare a SKILL.md file at their root stating the skill's name, description, and operating instructions.

/skills/zpd.title: Skills document non-trivial workflows
/skills/zpd.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide reference documentation for any non-trivial workflow, rather than relying on SKILL.md alone.

/skills/zpd.title: Skills verify themselves automatically
/skills/zpd.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide an automated, repeatable way to check their own correctness -- a test suite or a bundled validator -- rather than depending on manual review alone.

promises.name: _renders_only_generated_data
promises.title: The interface renders only previously generated data
promises.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to render only data previously generated by a skill's own tooling, and OUGHT NOT recompute domain logic -- validation, inheritance, trust, or scoring -- in the browser.

criteria.instructions: Confirm the interface reads a normalized JSON manifest per skill through its ResourceSource contract, and that no plugin view recomputes the skill's own derivations.

source.rationale: agency-ui/README.md states "The shell renders whatever the engines previously generated; it never re-derives anything," and read-only sources declare `determinism: static` with no `apply` capability.

promises.name: _declares_skill_manifest
promises.title: Skills declare a manifest
promises.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to declare a SKILL.md file at their root stating the skill's name, description, and operating instructions.

criteria.instructions: Confirm SKILL.md exists at the skill's root and its YAML front matter declares non-empty name and description fields.

source.rationale: Every skill in this repository is packaged and discovered through a SKILL.md front-matter contract; see ltp/SKILL.md, hypothesize/SKILL.md, and promisify/SKILL.md.

promises.name: _documents_references
promises.title: Skills document non-trivial workflows
promises.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide reference documentation for any non-trivial workflow, rather than relying on SKILL.md alone.

criteria.instructions: Confirm a references/ directory exists with at least one document per major workflow the SKILL.md delegates detail to.

source.rationale: Each existing skill's SKILL.md explicitly delegates workflow detail to a references/ directory rather than inlining it.

promises.name: _provides_automated_verification
promises.title: Skills verify themselves automatically
promises.statement: Subjects represented within this domain OUGHT to provide an automated, repeatable way to check their own correctness -- a test suite or a bundled validator -- rather than depending on manual review alone.

criteria.instructions: Run the package's bundled test suite or validator and confirm it completes and reports a pass/fail result.

source.rationale: ltp and hypothesize each ship a pytest suite exercising their own engine; a skill without an equivalent automated check depends entirely on manual review to catch regressions in its own tooling.

promises.name: _separates_capability_from_conclusion
promises.title: Capability, evidence, and conclusion stay separate
promises.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to keep capability status, evidence maturity, and scientific conclusion as three independent dimensions that are never conflated into one verdict.

criteria.instructions: Confirm SKILL.md and the engine keep the three dimensions structurally distinct, and that a passing test alone cannot promote a scientific conclusion.

source.rationale: hypothesize/SKILL.md states "Capability != evidence != conclusion" and "Passing tests can never promote a hypothesis."

promises.name: _generated_artifacts_are_derived
promises.title: Generated LTP artifacts are derived, not authored
promises.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to treat every file under a generated/ directory as derived output that tooling regenerates, and OUGHT NOT hand-edit it.

criteria.instructions: Confirm SKILL.md instructs against hand-editing generated/ output and that sync/check tooling exists to detect drift between the model and its generated projections.

source.rationale: ltp/SKILL.md states "Do not hand-edit anything under `generated/` -- it is machine-owned," and `ltp check` fails when a committed projection is stale or hand-edited.

promises.name: _promises_never_store_verdicts
promises.title: Promises never store a verdict
promises.statement: Subjects within this domain OUGHT to keep promise declarations free of any verdict, trust, or compliance state; only assessments may record kept/broken/trust.

criteria.instructions: Confirm the promise schema and validator reject a promise document containing status, verdict, kept, broken, trust, score, or compliance keys anywhere in its body.

source.rationale: promisify/SKILL.md's non-negotiable invariant #1 states a promise "never stores kept, broken, trust, compliance, or current truth," and `norms.py`'s FORBIDDEN_PROMISE_KEYS set enforces it at validation time.

assessments.rationale: SKILL.md states the three dimensions are never conflated, and core.py structurally separates capability_status, evidence_maturity, and conclusion in the derived manifest, only ever promoting conclusion from a qualified, preregistered study result.

evidence.summary: "Capability != evidence != conclusion." and "Passing tests can never promote a hypothesis."
`_derive_hypotheses` sets `capability_status`, `evidence_maturity`, and `conclusion` as three separate fields; conclusion only changes via `_is_qualified_scientific` (qualified AND preregistered).

assessments.rationale: hypothesize/SKILL.md exists and its front matter declares name and a description covering triggers and scope.

evidence.summary: Front matter declares `name: hypothesize` and a description explaining when to install, adopt, or extend the pattern.

assessments.rationale: hypothesize/references/ documents the adoption playbook, the publication model, and the tag vocabulary the SKILL.md delegates to.
hypothesize ships a pytest suite covering the engine, adapters, config, and a golden test against the Graphist fixture, run and passing at this revision.

Every installed plugin (ltp, hypothesize, promisify) registers a ResourceSource whose determinism is hard-coded "static" with no `apply` capability, so the shell cannot mutate or recompute what it renders; all three read one precomputed JSON manifest their skill's own tooling wrote.

evidence.summary: "The shell renders whatever the engines previously generated; it never re-derives anything."
`manifestSource()` hard-codes `determinism: "static"` and exposes no `apply` function, so a static source cannot be written to.

assessments.rationale: SKILL.md explicitly forbids hand-editing generated/ output, and `ltp check` re-derives the projections and fails on drift or a hand-edit.

evidence.summary: Do not hand-edit anything under `generated/` -- it is machine-owned.
`ltp check` re-renders the model in memory and exits non-zero if a committed generated/ file differs from the fresh render.

assessments.rationale: ltp/SKILL.md exists and its front matter declares name: ltp and a multi-sentence description covering triggers and scope.

evidence.summary: Front matter declares `name: ltp` and a description explaining when to use the skill (Goal Tree, Current/Future Reality Tree, Evaporating Cloud, Prerequisite Tree, Transition Tree, constraint diagnosis).

assessments.rationale: ltp/references/ contains one document per major workflow SKILL.md delegates to (model vocabulary, logical scrutiny, workflow stages, CLI and integration, and the diagnostics catalog).

evidence.summary: 6 files: model-and-vocabulary.md, logical-scrutiny.md, workflow.md, cli-and-integration.md, diagnostics.md, ltp-model.schema.json.

assessments.rationale: ltp ships a pytest suite covering the model, validators, renderers, migration, CLI, and schema generation, run and passing at this revision.
promisify/SKILL.md exists and its front matter declares name normative-promises and a description covering triggers and scope.

evidence.summary: Front matter declares `name: normative-promises` and a description naming norms, promises, assessments, and trust as triggers.

assessments.rationale: SKILL.md states the invariant and the validator enforces it structurally: FORBIDDEN_PROMISE_KEYS is checked against every key found anywhere in a Promise document's body, and the promise JSON Schema has no status/verdict/trust property at all.

evidence.summary: "A promise declares what OUGHT to be true. It never stores kept, broken, trust, compliance, or current truth."
FORBIDDEN_PROMISE_KEYS = {"status", "verdict", "kept", "broken", "trust", "score", "compliance"}, checked against every promise document's keys during validation.

assessments.rationale: promisify/references/ is the most extensively documented reference set in the repository, covering the ontology, domain resolution, assessments, trust, agent workflows, validation rules, and platform setup the SKILL.md delegates to.

evidence.summary: 10 files: ontology.md, repository-format.md, domain-resolution.md, assessments.md, trust.md, agent-workflows.md, validation-rules.md, platform-setup.md, platform-sources.md, extension-points.md.

assessments.rationale: No unit tests exist for norms.py's own logic (domain resolution, inheritance, assessment selection, or trust computation). The only automated fixtures under evals/ check whether the *skill's own description* triggers correctly for an agent -- a different property from whether norms.py's code behaves correctly. `norms.py validate` checks OTHER repositories' .norms/ data; it is a product feature of the tool, not a test of the tool's own implementation.

evidence.summary: No matches -- no pytest/unittest file exists for scripts/norms.py.

trigger-cases.yaml and workflow-cases.yaml are agent-triggering eval fixtures (shouldTrigger/shouldNotTrigger prompts), not unit tests of is_domain, effective_promises, calculate_trust, or the CLI commands.

views.name: default
views.description: Accept the repo-review agent's claims across the whole repository at the revision they were observed.
